visual_checks: Tab through interactive elements in the page — does moving focus to any element cause an unexpected change of context such as a new page loading, a form submitting, or a modal opening?
Check dropdown menus and select elements — does merely focusing on them (not activating or selecting) trigger navigation or page changes?
Look at links and buttons — does focusing on them (without clicking or pressing Enter) cause content to appear, disappear, or rearrange in a confusing way?
Check for any elements that launch pop-ups, redirect the page, or submit data when they receive keyboard focus
Examine embedded content (iframes, video players, widgets) — does focusing on them trigger autoplay, page scrolls, or layout shifts?
pass_conditions: No component initiates a change of context (page navigation, form submission, new window, focus moved to a different component) when it receives focus
Focus indicators appear on interactive elements when focused, but no automatic actions are triggered by focus alone
Dropdown menus and select elements only open or activate when the user deliberately interacts (click, Enter, Space) — not on focus
Tooltips or supplementary information that appears on focus do not constitute a change of context (supplementary info appearing is acceptable)
fail_conditions: Focusing on a link or button causes automatic navigation to another page without the user activating it
A form auto-submits when the user tabs to the submit button, before the user presses Enter or clicks
A dropdown select element navigates to a new URL immediately when it receives focus or when the user arrows through options
A modal dialog or pop-up window opens automatically when an element receives focus
Focus moving to an element causes a significant rearrangement of page content that disorients the user
na_conditions: Page has no interactive elements that can receive keyboard focus (no links, buttons, form controls, or custom widgets)
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Keyboard focus behavior could not be tested because screenshots are static and do not capture focus-triggered behavior
Dynamic behavior on focus could not be verified from DOM snapshot alone
common_mistakes: A select dropdown with an onchange handler that navigates on selection change, combined with keyboard behavior where arrowing through options triggers the change — this effectively causes context change on focus of each option
JavaScript onfocus handlers that open modal dialogs or redirect the page
Auto-advancing carousels or slideshows that activate when their controls receive focus
Custom widgets that move focus to a different part of the page when a component receives focus
Video or audio players that begin playback when their play button receives focus (before activation)

examples.pass: A navigation menu has dropdown sub-menus that only open when the user clicks or presses Enter on the parent item — tabbing to the parent item shows a focus ring but does not open the dropdown
examples.fail: A <select> element with navigation options uses onchange to redirect to a new page — when a keyboard user arrows through the options, the page changes before they can make a deliberate selection
examples.partial: Most interactive elements behave correctly on focus, but one widget in the sidebar launches a chat pop-up window automatically when its button receives keyboard focus

plain_meaning: Simply moving keyboard focus onto a control must not, on its own, trigger a significant change of context. A change of context means something major: opening a new window, navigating to a different page, substantially rearranging the page, or submitting a form. Merely showing a tooltip, revealing a submenu, or highlighting a focused element is NOT a change of context.
scope_applies_to: Keyboard focus landing on interactive components (links, buttons, form fields, custom widgets)
Programmatic focus placement that causes navigation or form submission
Custom focus handlers that open new windows or redirect on focus
Components whose onfocus handler submits a form, changes URL, or rearranges the page
Auto-advancing focus logic that moves focus beyond what the user expected
scope_does_not_apply_to: Hover or mouseover effects (focus is about keyboard/programmatic focus)
Showing a tooltip, popover, or submenu on focus without navigating
Visual focus indicator styling (that is SC 2.4.7)
Expanding a disclosure or accordion on click (click is input, not focus)
Dropdowns opening on focus when the dropdown is a child of the focused element and does not move focus elsewhere
Autocomplete suggestions appearing below a field while typing
borderline_calibration: A context change is BIG: new window, URL change, form submit, major layout shuffle. Small reveals are NOT context changes.
A popover or tooltip appearing on focus is NOT a violation.
A submenu expanding under a focused menu item is NOT a violation.
Flag only when focus alone demonstrably triggers navigation, a new window, or form submission without user intent.
If evidence comes only from suspicion (no captured behavior), prefer Not Enough Evidence.
